claim.content: Let \gamma denote the Sommerfeld coefficient defined by C_{\mathrm{el}}(T)=\gamma T (units J mol^{-1} K^{-2}). Let N(0) denote the quasiparticle density of states per mole at the Fermi level and let m^{*} denote the quasiparticle effective mass entering the low-energy Fermi-liquid description. In a conventional single-component Fermi liquid the relation \gamma=\tfrac{\pi^{2}}{3}k_{B}^{2}N(0) holds, and for bands whose density of states scales with effective mass one has N(0)\propto m^{*}, so that \gamma\propto m^{*}; therefore, to the extent that a Fermi-liquid description applies to the low-temperature electronic states under consideration, a measured change in \gamma(T) by a factor \sim 5 corresponds approximately to a fivefold change in the quasiparticle effective mass m^{*}.
